evidence.scientific_clarity: Clear 4×5 grouped bar chart with ±1-std error bars; OursModel visually distinguished as winner via darkest blue (#1a6fa8) and (*) marker in legend.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' explicitly set; font preference chain starts Arial→Helvetica (publication-appropriate); DejaVu Sans only as fallback #3.
evidence.palette: All-blue family palette (grey-blue to strong signal blue); no green/red for directional encoding; hatch patterns added for B&W/colorblind readers.
evidence.layout_density: Y-axis dynamically ranged from data (not 0–100); tight_layout used; 2-column legend inside axes; no excessive whitespace in rendered figure.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches figure exactly; OursModel marked (*) in both legend and caption; specific pp gains named; legend placed inside axes upper-left.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF header confirmed as %PDF-1.4 (75 KB); pdf.fonttype=42 ensures TrueType-embedded selectable text in vector output.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (75 KB), figure.png (142 KB, 300 dpi), caption.tex, gen_figure.py; bonus figure.svg (326 KB) also present.
evidence.caption_quality: Specific numerical finding stated (3.3–5.3 pp over Method-B); largest gains named (HumanEval +5.3 pp, GPQA +5.2 pp); explains error bars, hatching, winner notation, and n=5 seeds.

overall_strengths: All required output files present with non-trivial sizes; bonus figure.svg included; pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype='none' correctly enforced for editable vector text.
Caption includes a bolded quantitative finding with specific per-benchmark gains, and the all-blue palette with hatch overlays satisfies both colorblind-safety and B&W accessibility.
overall_weaknesses: Font preference chain includes DejaVu Sans as fallback #3; on systems without Arial or Helvetica the figure renders in the matplotlib default rather than a clean publication sans-serif.
Legend placed in upper-left inside the axes may visually overlap low-value bars on benchmarks where all methods score near the axis floor.
